name: The Blaze
descriptionSeed: The pure pole, and it is expanding. A light so total it has sterilized the land to glass and is still going — and the ones who live IN it are not a civilization anymore, they are a devotion. They have burned away everything that could cast a shadow, including most of what made them people, and they are radiantly happy about it. The Blazeborn do not go here. The Blaze comes to them, a little further every year.
encounterFlavor: The Blaze-devoted, who are joyous and empty; land that was a town last decade; something that survived in there and should not have.
questSeeds: The Blaze has taken a settlement and the Blazeborn are pretending it was a planned evacuation
Someone has walked INTO the Blaze on purpose and sent back a message

substrateSource.reason: The Blaze burns on lattice, not on fuel. It has been drawing it inward since before the Transition.
substrateSource.radiusNote: `radius` is in legacy map units; `radiusWorld` is RADII on the sphere and is the one mechanics should use. Chosen by sweep against the authored worldPos: at this scale all 26 sources resolve on the correct side of their region baseline and calibration drift stays ~0.05. Larger radii blanket a whole region (a source at ~55% strength everywhere inside it) and renormalization cancels the field flat — the failure CCode measured and reverted.

worldPosNote: Burns on lattice. The Radiant locus sits at the equator. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
